summary: Ready-mix concrete supply chains remain stable domestically, driven by regional quarries and cement plants across the Midwest and Southeast. However, tariff exposure on Portland cement imports from Canada and Mexico introduces moderate pricing risk for Q2–Q3 2026. Chinese clinker exports to Southeast Asia are increasing, creating alternative sourcing opportunities for Pacific Coast projects. Recommend locking in pricing with domestic producers before June tariff review window.

suppliers.name: Vulcan Materials – Ready Mix Division
suppliers.supply_route_description: Quarried limestone and shale from regional Alabama and Georgia operations. Cement sourced from Vulcan's own plants. Delivery radius 150 miles per batch plant.

suppliers.name: Martin Marietta Materials
suppliers.supply_route_description: Crushed stone and aggregates from 200+ quarries across 27 states. Ready-mix operations in major MSAs. Portland cement sourced from domestic kilns.

suppliers.name: CEMEX USA
suppliers.supply_route_description: Parent company (Mexico) supplies clinker via Gulf Coast ports. US operations blend and distribute. Tariff risk on Mexican imports moderately elevated.
suppliers.risk_notes: Clinker imports from parent facilities in Mexico subject to potential 2026 tariff revisions. Pricing volatility risk through mid-year.

suppliers.name: Siam City Cement (SCC) – Export Division
suppliers.supply_route_description: Clinker and OPC exported via Port of Laem Chabang to US West Coast ports. 45–60 day lead time. Competitive on large-volume Pacific Coast projects.

suppliers.name: Dangote Cement – International Export
suppliers.supply_route_description: Sub-Saharan Africa's largest cement producer exports OPC via Atlantic routing. 60–75 day lead time. Best suited for East Coast port delivery on large infrastructure projects.
suppliers.risk_notes: Port congestion at Apapa terminal intermittently causes 10–15 day delays. Logistics risk moderate. Quality meets ASTM standards per third-party testing.

news_items.title: USTR Proposes 15% Tariff Increase on Canadian Cement Imports Effective June 1
Ready-Mix Prices Up 4.2% YTD as Diesel Surcharges Rise
Southeast US Concrete Supply Tightens Ahead of Hurricane Season Infrastructure Push
CEMEX Q1 Earnings: North American Volume Up, Pricing Guidance Raised
